zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes於大約1250年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯範恩斯時，她的父親Guillaume de Fiennes II12歲，母親Blanche de Brienne5歲。她於1285年9月與Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer在英國賀福爾維格莫結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1334年2月15日在英國賀福爾維格莫享年85歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾維格莫維格莫 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes于大约1250年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯範恩斯时，她的父亲Guillaume de Fiennes II12岁，母亲Blanche de Brienne5岁。她于1285年9月与Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer在英国賀福爾維格莫结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1334年2月15日在英国賀福爾維格莫享年85岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾維格莫維格莫 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Fiennes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Finnis
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: locative name from Fiennes (Pas-de-Calais).  See also  Filtness  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes was born about 1250, in Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, her father, Guillaume de Fiennes II, was 12 and her mother, Blanche de Brienne, was 5. She married Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer in September 1285, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 15 February 1334, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 85, and was buried in Wigmore Abbey, Wigmore, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes est née environ 1250, à Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Guillaume de Fiennes II, avait 12 ans et sa mère, Blanche de Brienne, avait 5 ans. Elle a épousé Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer en septembre 1285, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 15 février 1334, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterrée à Wigmore Abbey, Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat im September 1285 in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 15. Februar 1334 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England und wurde in Wigmore Abbey, Wigmore, Herefordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Eleanor de Fiennesが約1250年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイスフィーンスで生まれた時，父Guillaume de Fiennes IIは12歳，母Blanche de Brienneは5歳であった。1285年9月にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーでEdmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1334年2月15日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで85歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーウィグモー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Louplande, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Fra: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 루플랜드
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes(이)가 대략 1250년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 피네스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume de Fiennes II(은)는 12세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Blanche de Brienne(은)는 5세였다. 그녀는 1285년 9월 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1334년 2월 15일 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어 위그모어 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes nasceu aproximadamente 1250, em Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Guillaume de Fiennes II, tinha 12 anos e sua mãe, Blanche de Brienne, tinha 5 anos. Ela casou-se com Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer em setembro de 1285, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 15 de fevereiro de 1334, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos, e foi sepultada em Wigmore Abbey, Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Louplande, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Fra: лоупланд, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes родилась около 1250 г. в местности фиеннесь, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume de Fiennes II было 12 лет, а матери, которую звали Blanche de Brienne, 5. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer сентябрь 1285 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 15 февраля 1334 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности вигмор эбби, вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.Louplande, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Fra: Louplande, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes nació aproximadamente 1250, en Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Guillaume de Fiennes II, tenía 12 años y su mamá, Blanche de Brienne, tenía 5 años. Se casó con Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer en septiembre de 1285, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 15 de febrero de 1334, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultada en Wigmore Abbey, Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
